verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die; but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After showing how great the reward of a spiritual life is, and that it maketh Christ to dwell in us, and that it quickeneth our mortal bodies, and wingeth them to heaven, and rendereth the way of virtue easier, he next fitly introduces an exhortation to this purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Therefore” we ought “not to live after the flesh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this is not what he says, for he words it in a much more striking and powerful way, thus, “we are debtors to the Spirit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For saying, “we are debtors not to the flesh,” indicates this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is a point he is everywhere giving proof of, that what God hath done for us is not matter of debt, but of mere grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But after this, what we do is no longer matter of free-will offering, but of debt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he saith, “Ye are bought with a price, be not ye the servants of men” (1 Cor. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 23); and when he writes, “Ye are not your own” (ib. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 19); and again in another passage he calls these selfsame things to their mind, in these words, “If (most mss. om. “if”) One died for all, then all died that they should not henceforth live unto themselves.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it is to establish this that he says here also, “We are debtors;” then since he said we are “not” debtors “to the flesh,” lest you should again take him to be speaking against the nature of the flesh, he does not leave speaking, but proceeds, “to live after the flesh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there are many things which we do owe it, as giving it food, warmth, and rest, medicine when out of health, clothing, and a thousand other attentions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: To prevent your supposing then that it is this ministration he is for abrogating when he says, “We are not debtors to the flesh,” he explains it by saying, “to live after the flesh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the care that I am for abrogating is, he means, that which leadeth to sin, as I should be for its having what is healing to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he shows further on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he says, “Make not provision for the flesh,” he does not pause at this, but adds, “to fulfil the lusts thereof.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this instruction he gives us here also, meaning, Let it have attention shown it indeed, for we do owe it this, yet let us not live according to the flesh, that is, let us not make it the mistress of our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it must be the follower, not the leader, and it is not it that must regulate our life, but the laws of the Spirit must it receive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Having then defined this point, and having proved that we are debtors to the Spirit, to show next for what benefits it is that we are debtors, he does not speak of those past (a thing which serves as a most striking proof of his judgment), but those which were to come; although even the former were enough for the purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet still he does not set them down in the present case, or mention even those unspeakable blessings, but the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For a benefit once for all conferred does not, for the most part, draw men on so much as one which is expected, and is to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After adding this then, he first uses the pains and ills that come of living after the flesh, to put them in fear, in the following words; “For if ye live after the flesh ye shall die,” so intimating to us that deathless death, punishment, and vengeance in hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather if one were to look accurately into this, such an one is, even in this present life, dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this we have made clear to you in the last discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But if ye through the Spirit, do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see that it is not the essence of the body whereof we are discoursing, but the deeds of the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he does not say, “if ye through the Spirit do mortify” the essence “of the body,” but “the deeds of” it, and these not all deeds, but such as are evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is plain in what follows: for if ye do this, “ye shall live,” he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And how is it in the nature of things for this to be, if it was all deeds that his language applied to? for seeing and hearing and speaking and walking are deeds of the body; and if we mortify these, we shall be so far from living, that we shall have to suffer the punishment of a manslayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What sort of deeds then does he mean us to mortify?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Those which tend toward wickedness, those which go after vice, which there is no other way of mortifying save through the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For by killing yourself you may put an end to the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this you have no right to do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But to these (you can put an end) by the Spirit only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if This be present, all the billows are laid low, and the passions cower under It, and nothing can exalt itself against us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So you see how it is on things to come, as I said before, that he grounds his exhortations to us, and shows that we are debtors not owing to what has been already done only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the advantage of the Spirit is not this only, that He hath set us free from our former sins, but that He rendereth us impregnable against future ones, and counts us worthy of the immortal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then, to state another reward also, he proceeds:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now this is again a much greater honor than the first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is why he does not say merely, As many as live by the Spirit of God, but, “as many as are led by the Spirit of God,” to show that he would have Him use such power over our life as a pilot doth over a ship, or a charioteer over a pair of horses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it is not the body only, but the soul itself too, that he is for setting under reins of this sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he would not have even that independent, but place its authority also under the power of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For lest through a confidence in the Gift of the Font they should turn negligent of their conversation after it, he would say, that even supposing you receive baptism, yet if you are not minded to be “led by the Spirit” afterwards, you lose the dignity bestowed upon you, and the pre-eminence of your adoption.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This is why he does not say, As many as have received the Spirit, but, “as many as are led by the Spirit,” that is, as many as live up to this all their life long, “they are the sons of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then since this dignity was given to the Jews also, for it says, “I said ye are Gods, and all of you children of the Most High” (Ps. lxxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 6); and again, “I have nourished and brought up children” (Is. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 2); and so, “Israel is My first-born” (Ex. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 22); and Paul too says, “Whose is the adoption” (Rom. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 4)—he next asserts the great difference between the latter and the former honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For though the names are the same, he means, still, the things are not the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And of these points he gives a clear demonstration, by introducing a comparison drawn both from the persons so advanced (κατορθούντων) and from what was given them, and from what was to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And first he shows what they of old had given them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then was this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “A spirit of bondage:” and so he thus proceeds,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then not staying to mention that which stands in contradistinction to bondage, that is, the spirit of freedom, he has named what is far greater, that of adoption, through which he at the same time brings in the other, saying, “But ye have received the Spirit of adoption.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this is plain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But what the spirit of bondage may be, is not so plain, and there is need of making it clearer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now what he says is so far from being clear, that it is in fact very perplexing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the people of the Jews did not receive the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then is his meaning here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is the letter he giveth this name to, for spiritual it was, and so he called the Law spiritual also, and the water from the Rock, and the Manna.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For they did eat,” he says, “of the same spiritual meat, and all drank of the same spiritual drink.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And to the Rock he gives this name, when he says, “For they drank of that spiritual Rock which followed them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now it is because all the rites then wrought were above nature that he calls them spiritual, and not because those who then partook of them received the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in what sense were those letters, letters of bondage?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Set before yourself the whole dispensation, and then you will have a clear view of this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For recompenses were with them close at hand, and the reward followed forthwith, being at once proportionate, and like a kind of daily ration given to domestic servants, and terrors in abundance came to their height before their eyes, and their purifications concerned their bodies, and their continency extended but to their actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But with us it is not so, since the imagination even and the conscience getteth purged out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He does not say, “Thou shalt do no murder,” only, but even thou shalt not be angry: so too, it is not, “Thou shalt not commit adultery,” but thou shalt not look unchastely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So that it is not to be from fear of present punishment, but out of desire towards Himself, that both our being habitually virtuous, and all our single good deeds are to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Neither doth he promise a land flowing with milk and honey, but maketh us joint-heir with the Only-Begotten, so making us by every means stand aloof from things present, and promising to give such things especially as are worth the acceptance of men made sons of God, nothing, that is, of a sensible kind or corporeal, but spiritual all of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so they, even if they had the name of sons, were but as slaves; but we as having been made free, have received the adoption, and are waiting for Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with them He discoursed through the intervention of others, with us by Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And all that they did was through the impulse of fear, but the spiritual act through a coveting and a vehement desire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this they show by the fact of their overstepping the commandments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They, as hirelings and obstinate persons, so never left murmuring: but these do all for the pleasing of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So too they blasphemed when they had benefits done them: but we are thankful at being jeoparded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if there be need of punishing both of us upon our sinning, even in this case the difference is great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not on being stoned and branded and maimed by the priests, as they were, that we are brought round.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But it is enough for us to be cast out from our Father’s table, and to be out of sight for certain days.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with the Jews the honor of adoption was one of name only, but here the reality followed also, the cleansing of Baptism, the giving of the Spirit, the furnishing of the other blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And there are several other points besides, which go to show our high birth and their low condition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After intimating all these then by speaking of the Spirit, and fear, and the adoption, he gives a fresh proof again of having the Spirit of adoption.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That “we cry, Abba, Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And how great this is, the initiated know (St. Cyr.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 276, O.T.), being with good reason bidden to use this word first in the Prayer of the initiated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then, it may be said, did not they also call God Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Dost thou not hear Moses, when he says, “Thou desertedst the God that begot thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Deut. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Dost thou not hear Malachi reproaching them, and saying, that “one God formed you,” and there is “one Father of you all?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still, if these words and others besides are used, we do not find them anywhere calling God by the name, or praying in this language.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But we all, priests and laymen, rulers and ruled, are ordered to pray herein.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is the first language we give utterance to, after those marvellous throes, and that strange and unusual mode of labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If in any other instances they so called Him, that was only of their own mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But those in the state of grace do it through being moved by the in-working of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as there is a Spirit of Wisdom, after which they that were unwise became wise, and this discloses itself in their teaching: and a Spirit of Power there is, whereby the feeble raised up the dead, and drove out devils; a Spirit also of the gift of healing, and a Spirit of prophecy, and a Spirit of tongues, so also a Spirit of adoption.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And as we know the Spirit of prophecy, in that he who hath it foretelleth things to come, not speaking of his own mind, but moved by the Grace; so too is the Spirit of adoption, whereby he that is gifted with it calleth God, Father, as moved by the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wishing to express this as a most true descent, he used also the Hebrew tongue, for he does not say only, “Father,” but “Abba, Father,” which name is a special sign of true-born children to their fathers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After mentioning then the diversity resulting from their conversation, that resulting from the grace which had been given, and that from their freedom, he brings forward another demonstration of the superiority which goes with this adoption.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now of what kind is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “The Spirit Itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not from the language merely, he says, that I make my assertion, but from the cause out of which the language has its birth; since it is from the Spirit suggesting it that we so speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this in another passage he has put into plainer words, thus: “God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is that, “Spirit beareth witness with spirit?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Comforter, he means, with that Gift, which is given unto us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not of the Gift alone that it is the voice, but of the Comforter also who gave the Gift, He Himself having taught us through the Gift so to speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when the “Spirit beareth witness” what farther place for doubtfulness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if it were a man, or angel, or archangel, or any other such power that promised this, then there might be reason in some doubting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when it is the Highest Essence that bestoweth this Gift, and “beareth witness” by the very words He bade us use in prayer, who would doubt any more of our dignity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For not even when the Emperor elects any one, and proclaims in all men’s hearing the honor done him, does anybody venture to gainsay.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And if children, then heirs.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Observe how he enhances the Gift by little and little.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since it is a possible case to be children, and yet not become heirs (for it is not by any means all children that are heirs), he adds this besides—that we are heirs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the Jews, besides their not having the same adoption as we, were also cast out from the inheritance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “He will miserably destroy those wicked men, and will let out the vineyard to other husbandmen” (Matt. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 41): and before this, He said that “many shall come from the East and from the West, and shall sit down with Abraham, but the children of the Kingdom shall be cast out.” (ib. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But even here he does not pause, but sets down something even greater than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What may this be then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That we are heirs of God; and so he adds, “heirs of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is more still, that we are not simply heirs, but also “joints heirs with Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Observe how ambitious he is of bringing us near to the Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since it is not all children that are heirs, he shows that we are both children and heirs; next, as it is not all heirs that are heirs to any great amount, he shows that we have this point with us too, as we are heirs of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Again, since it were possible to be God’s heir, but in no sense “joint heir with” the Only-Begotten, he shows that we have this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And consider his wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after throwing the distasteful part into a short compass, when he was saying what was to become of such as “live after the flesh,” for instance, that they “shall die,” when he comes to the more soothing part, he leadeth forth his discourse into a large room, and so expands it on the recompense of rewards, and in pointing out that the gifts too are manifold and great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if even the being a child were a grace unspeakable, just think how great a thing it is to be heir!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if this be great, much more is it to be “joint heir.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then to show that the Gift is not of grace only, and to give at the same time a credibility to what he says, he proceeds, “If so be that we suffer with Him, that we may be also glorified together.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If, he would say, we be sharers with Him in what is painful, much more shall it be so in what is good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He who bestowed such blessings upon those who had wrought no good, how, when He seeth them laboring and suffering so much, shall he do else than give them greater requital?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Having then shown that the thing was a matter of return, to make men give credit to what was said, and prevent any from doubting, he shows further that it has the virtue of a gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The one he showed, that what was said might gain credit even with those that doubted, and that the receivers of it might not feel ashamed as being evermore receiving salvation for nought; and the other, that you might see that God outdoeth the toils by His recompenses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And the one he has shown in the words, “If so be that we suffer with Him, that we may be also glorified together.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the other in proceeding to add;

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “The sufferings of the present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in (Gr. εἰς) us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In what went before, he requires of the spiritual man the correcting of his habits (Mar. and 6 mss. passions), where he says, “Ye are not debtors to live after the flesh,” that such an one, for instance, should be above lust, anger, money, vainglory, grudging.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But here having reminded them of the whole gift, both as given and as to come, and raised him up aloft with hopes, and placed him near to Christ, and showed him to be a joint-heir of the Only-Begotten; he now leads him forth with confidence even to dangers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For to get the better of the evil affections in us, is not the same thing with bearing up under those trials, scourges, famine, plunderings, bonds, chains, executions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For these last required much more of a noble and vigorous spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And observe how he at once allays and rouses the spirit of the combatants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after he had shown that the rewards were greater than the labors, he both exhorts to greater efforts, and yet will not let them be elated, as being still outdone by the crowns given in requital.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in another passage he says, “For our light affliction, which is but for a moment, worketh a far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory” (2 Cor. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 17): it being the deeper sort of persons he was then speaking to.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here, however, he does not allow that the afflictions were light; but still he mingles comfort with them by the compensation which good things to come afford, in the words, “For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared,” and he does not say, with the rest (ἄνεσιν) that is to come, but what is much greater, “with the glory which is to come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it does not follow, that where rest is there is glory; but that where glory is there is rest, does follow: then as he had said that it is to come, he shows that it already is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he does not say, that which is to be, but “which shall be revealed in us,” as if already existing but unrevealed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As also in another place he said in clearer words, “Our life is hid with Christ in God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Be then of a good heart about it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For already hath it been prepared, and awaiteth thy labors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if it vexes you that it is yet to come, rather let this very thing rejoice you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is owing to its being great and unutterable, and transcending our present condition, that it is stored up there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so he has not put barely “the sufferings of this present time,” but he speaks so as to show that it is not in quality only, but in quantity also, that the other life has the advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For these sufferings, whatever they are, are attached to our present life; but the blessings to come reach themselves out over ages without end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And since he had no way of giving a particular description of these, or of putting them before us in language, he gives them a name from what seems to be specially an object of desire with us, “glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the summit of blessings and the sum of them, this seems to be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And to urge the hearer on in another way also, he gives a loftiness to his discourse by the mention of the creation, gaining two points by what he is next saying, the contempt of things present, and the desire of things to come, and a third beside these, or rather the first, is the showing how the human race is cared for on God’s part and in what honor He holds our nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And besides this, all the doctrines of the philosophers, which they had framed for themselves about this world, as a sort of cobweb or child’s mound, he throws down with this one doctrine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that these things may stand in a clearer light, let us hear the Apostle’s own language.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For the earnest expectation of the creation waiteth,” he says, “for the revelation of the sons of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the creation was made subject to vanity, not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subjected the same in hope.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And the meaning is something of this kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The creation itself is in the midst of its pangs, waiting for and expecting these good things whereof we have just now spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “earnest expectation” (ἀποκαραδοκία, looking out) implies expecting intensely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so his discourse becomes more emphatic, and he personifies this whole world as the prophets also do, when they introduce the floods clapping their hands, and little hills leaping, and mountains skipping, not that we are to fancy them alive, or ascribe any reasoning power to them, but that we may learn the greatness of the blessings, so great as to reach even to things without sense also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The very same thing they do many times also in the case of afflicting things, since they bring in the vine lamenting, and the wine too, and the mountains, and the boardings of the Temple howling, and in this case too it is that we may understand the extremity of the evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is then in imitation of these that the Apostle makes a living person of the creature here, and says that it groaneth and travaileth: not that he heard any groan conveyed from the earth and heaven to him, but that he might show the exceeding greatness of the good things to come; and the desire of freedom from the ills which now pervaded them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For the creature was made subject to vanity, not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subjected the same.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What is the meaning of, “the creation was made subject to vanity?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why that it became corruptible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what cause, and on what account?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: On account of thee, O man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since thou hast taken a body mortal and liable to suffering, the earth too hath received a curse, and brought forth thorns and thistles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that the heaven, when it is waxen old along with the earth, is to change afterwards to a better portion (λἥξιν v. p.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 384) hear from the Prophet in his words; “Thou, O Lord, from the beginning hast founded the earth, and the heavens are the work of Thy hands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They shall perish, but thou shalt endure; and they all shall wax old as doth a garment, and as a cloak shalt Thou fold them up, and they shall be changed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Isaiah too declares the same, when he says, “Look to the heaven above, and upon the earth beneath, for the heavens are as a firmament of smoke, and the earth shall wax old like a garment, and they that dwell therein shall perish in like manner.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now you see in what sense the creation is “in bondage to vanity,” and how it is to be freed from the ruined state.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the one says, “Thou shalt fold them up as a garment, and they shall be changed;” and Isaiah says, “and they that dwell therein shall perish in like manner,” not of course meaning an utter perishing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For neither do they that dwell therein, mankind, that is, undergo such an one, but a temporary one, and through it they are changed into an incorruptible (1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 53) state, and so therefore will the creature be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And all this he showed by the way, by his saying “in like manner” (2 Pet. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 13), which Paul also says farther on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: At present, however, he speaks about the bondage itself, and shows for what reason it became such, and gives ourselves as the cause of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Was it harshly treated on another’s account?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: By no means, for it was on my account that it was made.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What wrong then is done it, which was made for my sake, when it suffereth these things for my correction?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or, indeed, one has no need to moot the question of right and wrong at all in the case of things void of soul and feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But Paul, since he had made it a living person, makes use of none of these topics I have mentioned, but another kind of language, as desiring to comfort the hearer with the utmost advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And of what kind is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What have you to say? he means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was evil intreated for thy sake, and became corruptible; yet it has had no wrong done it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For incorruptible will it be for thy sake again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then is the meaning of “in hope.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when he says, it was “not willingly” that it was made subject, it is not to show that it is possessed of judgment that he says so, but that you may learn that the whole is brought about by Christ’s care, and this is no achievement of its own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And now say in what hope?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That the creature itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now what is this creation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Not thyself alone, but that also which is thy inferior, and partaketh not of reason or sense, this too shall be a sharer in thy blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “it shall be freed,” he says, “from the bondage of corruption,” that is, it shall no longer be corruptible, but shall go along with the beauty given to thy body; just as when this became corruptible, that became corruptible also; so now it is made incorruptible, that also shall follow it too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And to show this he proceeds. (εἰς) “Into the glorious liberty of the children of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That is, because of their liberty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as a nurse who is bringing up a king’s child, when he has come to his father’s power, does herself enjoy the good things along with him, thus also is the creation, he means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see how in all respects man takes the lead, and that it is for his sake that all things are made.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See how he solaces the struggler, and shows the unspeakable love of God toward man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For why, he would say, dost thou fret at thy temptations? thou art suffering for thyself, the creation for thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Nor does he solace only, but also shows what he says to be trustworthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if the creation which was made entirely for thee is “in hope,” much more oughtest thou to be, through whom the creation is to come to the enjoyment of those good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thus men (3 mss. fathers) also when a son is to appear at his coming to a dignity, clothe even the servants with a brighter garment, to the glory of the son; so will God also clothe the creature with incorruption for the glorious liberty of the children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Observe, how he shames the hearer, saying almost, Be not thou worse than the creation, neither find a pleasure in resting in things present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Not only ought we not to cling to them, but even to groan over the delay of our departure hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if the creation doth this, much more oughtest thou to do so, honored with reason as thou art.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But as this was not yet enough to force their attention, he proceeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And not only they, but ourselves also, which have the first-fruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That is, having had a taste of the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even if any should be quite stone hard, he means what has been given already is enough to raise him up, and draw him off from things present, and to wing him after things to come in two ways, both by the greatness of the things that are given, and by the fact that, great and numerous as they are, they are but first-fruits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if the first-fruits be so great that we are thereby freed even from our sins, and attain to righteousness and sanctification, and that those of that time both drave out devils, and raised the dead by their shadow (Acts v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 15), or garments (ib. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 12), consider how great the whole must be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if the creation, devoid as it is of mind and reason, and though in ignorance of these things, yet groaneth, much more should we.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Next, that he may give the heretics no handle, or seem to be disparaging our present world, we groan, he says, not as finding fault with the present system, but through a desire of those greater things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he shows in the words, “Waiting for the adoption.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What dost thou say, let me hear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thou didst insist on it at every turn, and didst cry aloud, that we were already made sons, and now dost thou place this good thing among hopes, writing that we must needs wait for it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now it is to set this right by the sequel that he says, “to wit, the redemption of our body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That is, the perfect glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Our lot indeed is at present uncertainty to our last breath, since many of us that were sons have become dogs and prisoners.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if we decease with a good hope, then is the gift unmovable, and clearer, and greater, having no longer any change to fear from death and sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then therefore will the grace be secure, when our body shall be freed from death and its countless ailments (or passions).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is full redemption (ἀπολύτρωσις), not a redemption only, but such, that we shall never again return to our former captivity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that thou mayest not be perplexed at hearing so much of glory without getting any distinct knowledge of it, he partially exposes to thy view the things to come, setting before thee the change of thy body (Gr. changing thy body), and along with it the change of the whole creation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he has put in a clearer light in another passage, where he says, “Who shall change our vile body, that it may be fashioned like unto His glorious Body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in another place again he writes and says, “But when this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But to show, that with the corruption of the body the constitution of the things of this life will also come to an end, he wrote again elsewhere, “For the fashion of this world passeth away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (1 Cor. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For we are saved by hope,” he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now since he had dwelt upon the promise of the things to come, and this seemed to pain the weaker hearer, if the blessings are all matter of hope; after proving before that they are surer than things present and visible, and discoursing at large on the gifts already given, and showing that we have received the first fruits of those good things, lest we should seek our all in this world, and be traitors to the nobility that faith gives us, he says, “For we are (Gr. were) saved by hope.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is about what he means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: We are not to seek our all in this life, but to have hope also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is the only gift that we brought in to God, believing Him in what He promised shall come, and it was by this way alone we were saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If then we lose this hope, we have lost all that was of our own contributing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For I put you this question, he would say, Wert thou not liable for countless sins? wert thou not in despair? wert thou not under sentence? were not all out of heart about thy salvation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then saved thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was thy hoping in God alone, and trusting to Him about His promises and gifts, and nothing besides hadst thou to bring in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If it was this then that saved thee, hold it fast now also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that which afforded thee so great blessings, to a certainty will not deceive thee in regard to things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For in that it found thee dead, and ruined, and a prisoner, and an enemy, and yet made thee a friend, and a son, and a freeman, and righteous, and a joint-heir, and yielded such great things as no one ever expected even, how, after such munificence and attachment, will it betray thee in what is to follow?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Say not to me, hopes again! expectations again! faith again!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is in this way thou wert saved from the beginning, and this dowry was the only one that thou didst bring in to the Bridegroom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hold it then fast and keep it: for if thou demandest to have everything in this world, thou hast lost that well-doing of thine, through which thou didst become bright, and this is why he proceeds to say, “But hope that is seen is not hope; for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 25.—“But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That is, if thou art to be looking for everything in this world, what need is there for hope?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What is hope then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is feeling confidence in things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What great demand then doth God make upon thee, since He Himself giveth thee blessings quite entire from His own stores?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: One thing only, hope, He asks of thee, that thou too mayest have somewhat of thine own to contribute toward thy salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he intimates in what he proceeds with: “For if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then God crowneth him that undergoes labors, and hardnesses, and countless toils, so doth He him that hopeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the name of patience belongs to hard work and much endurance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet even this He hath granted to the man that hopeth, that He might solace the wearied soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And then to show that for this light task we enjoy abundant aid, he proceeds:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the one point is thy own, that of patience, but the other comes of the Spirit’s furnishings, Who also cherisheth (Gr. anointeth) thee unto this hope, and through it again lighteneth thy labors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then that thou mightest know that it is not in thy labors only and dangers that this grace standeth by thee, but even in things the most easy seemingly, it worketh with thee, and on all occasions bears its part in the alliance, he proceeds to say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For we know not what we should pray for as we ought.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he said to show the Spirit’s great concern about us, and also to instruct them not to think for certainty that those things are desirable which to man’s reasonings appear so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since it was likely that they, when they were scourged, and driven out, and suffering grievances without number, should be seeking a respite, and ask this favor of God, and think it was advantageous to them, by no means (he says) suppose that what seem blessings to you really are so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For we need the Spirit’s aid even to do this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So feeble is man, and such a nothing by himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is why he says, “For we know not what we should pray for as we ought.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In order that the learner might not feel any shame at his ignorance, he does not say, ye know not, but, “we know not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And that he did not say this merely to seem moderate, he plainly shows from other passages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he desired in his prayers unceasingly to see Rome.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet the time when he obtained it was not at once when he desired it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for “the thorn” that was given him “in the flesh” (2 Cor. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 8), that is the dangers, he often besought God, and was entirely unsuccessful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so was Moses, who in the Old Testament prays to see Palestine (Deut. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 26), and Jeremiah when he made supplication for the Jews (Jer. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 1), and Abraham when he interceded for the people of Sodom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But the Spirit Itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This statement is not clear, owing to the cessation of many of the wonders which then used to take place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore I must needs inform you of the state of things at that time, and in this way the rest of the subject will be cleared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What therefore was the state of things then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: God did in those days give to all that were baptized certain excellent gifts, and the name that these had was spirits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “the spirits of the Prophets,” it says, “are subject to the prophets.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (1 Cor. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And one had the gift of prophecy and foretold things to come; and another of wisdom, and taught the many; and another of healings, and cured the sick; and another of miracles, and raised the dead; another of tongues, and spoke different languages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with all these there was also a gift of prayer, which also was called a spirit, and he that had this prayed for all the people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since we are ignorant of much that is profitable for us and ask things that are not profitable, the gift of prayer came into some particular person of that day, and what was profitable for all the whole Church alike, he was the appointed person to ask for in behalf of all, and the instructor of the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Spirit then is the name that he gives here to the grace of this character, and the soul that receiveth the grace, and intercedeth to God, and groaneth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he that was counted worthy of such grace as this, standing with much compunction, and with many mental groanings falling before God, asked the things that were profitable for all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And of this the Deacon of the present day is a symbol when he offers up the prayers for the people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then is what Paul means when he says, “the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings that cannot be uttered.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But He that searcheth the hearts.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see that it is not about the Comforter that he is speaking, but about the spiritual heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since if this were not so, he ought to have said, “He that searcheth” the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that thou mayest learn that the language is meant of a spiritual man, who has the gift of prayer, he proceeds, “And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit,” that is, of the spiritual man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Not (he means) that he informs God as if ignorant, but this is done that we may learn to pray for proper things, and to ask of God what is pleasing to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is what the “according to God” is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so this was with a view to solace those that came to Him, and to yield them excellent instruction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He that furnished the gifts, and gave besides blessings without number, was the Comforter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence it says, “all these things worketh one and the self-same Spirit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (1 Cor. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it is for our instruction that this takes place, and to show the love of the Spirit, it condescendeth even to this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it is from this that the person praying getteth heard, because the prayer is made “according to the will of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see from how many points he instructs them in the love that was shown them and the honor that was done them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is there that God hath not done for us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The world He hath made corruptible for us, and again for us incorruptible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He suffered His Prophets to be ill-treated for our sake, sent them into captivity for us, let them fall into the furnace, and undergo ills without number.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Nay, He made them prophets for us, and the Apostles also He made for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He gave up for us His Only-Begotten, He punisheth the devil for us, He hath seated us on the Right Hand, He was reproached for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For the reproaches of them that reproached thee,” it says, “fell upon me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet still, when we are drawing back after so great favor, He leaveth us not, but again entreats, and on our account inciteth others to entreat for us, that He may show us favor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so it was with Moses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For to him He says, “Let Me alone, that I may blot them out” (Ex. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 10), that He might drive him upon supplicating on their behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And now He doth the same thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence He gave the gift of prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this He doth, not as Himself standing in need of entreaty, but that we might not, from being saved without effort (ἁπλὥς), grow indifferent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this cause it is on account of David, and of this person and that, He often says, that He is reconciled with them, to establish again this very thing, that the reconciliation may be with all due formality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still He would have looked more loving toward man, if it had not been through this and the other prophet, but of Himself, that He told them that He ceased to be wroth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the reason of His not holding to that point was, that this ground of reconciliation might not become an occasion for listlessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore to Jeremiah also He said, “Pray not for this people, for I will not hear thee” (Jer. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 14), not as wishing to stop his praying (for He earnestly longeth for our salvation), but to terrify them: and this the prophet also seeing did not cease praying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And that you may see that it was not through a wish to turn him from it, but to shame them that He said this, hear what it says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Seest thou not what these are doing?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 6, not verbally from LXX.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And when He says to the city “Though thou wash thee with nitre, and take thee much soap (Gr. herb), yet thou art stained before Me” (Jer. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 22), it is not that He may cast them into despair that He so speaks, but that He may rouse them to repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as in the case of the Ninevites, by giving the sentence without limitation, and holding out no good hope, He scared them the more, and led them to repentance, so He doth here also, both to rouse them, and to render the prophet more venerated, that in this way at least they may hear him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then, since they kept on in a state of incurable madness, and were not to be sobered even by the rest being carried away, he first exhorts them to remain there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when they kept not up to this, but deserted to Egypt, this indeed He allowed them, but requires of them not to desert to irreligion as well as to Egypt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when they did not comply in this either, He sendeth the prophet along with them, so that they might not after all suffer total wreck.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since they did not follow Him when He called, He next followeth them to discipline them, and hinder their being hurried further into vice, and as a father full of affection does a child who takes all treatment in the same peevish way, conducting him about everywhere with himself, and following him about.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This was the reason why He sent not Jeremiah only into Egypt, but also Ezekiel into Babylon, and they did not refuse to go.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when they found their Master love the people exceedingly, they continued themselves to do so likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Much as if a right-minded servant were to take compassion upon an intractable son when he saw his father grieving and lamenting about him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what was there that they did not suffer for them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They were sawn asunder, they were driven out, they were reproached, they were stoned, they underwent numberless grievances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And after all this they would run back to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Samuel, for instance, ceased not to mourn for Saul, miserably insulted as he was by him, and injured irreparably.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still he held none of these things in remembrance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for the people of the Jews, Jeremiah has composed Lamentations in writing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And when the general of the Persians had given him liberty to dwell securely, and with perfect freedom, wherever he pleased, he preferred above dwelling at home the affliction of the people, and their hard durance in a strange land.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So Moses left the palace and the sort of living herein, and hasted to be among their calamities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And Daniel abode for twenty days following without food, pinching himself with the most severe fast, that he might reconcile God to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And the three Children too, when in the furnace, and so fierce a fire, put up a supplication for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was not on their account that they were grieved, as they were saved; but since they considered that then was the time for the greatest boldness of speech, they consequently prayed in their behalf; hence too they said, “In a contrite heart and an humble spirit let us be accepted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For them Joshua also rent his garments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For them Ezekiel too wailed and lamented when he saw them cut down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And Jeremy said, “Let me alone, I will weep bitterly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And before this, when he did not venture openly to pray for a remittance of their sad estate, he sought for some limited period, when he says, “How long, O, Lord?” (ib. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For full of affectionateness is the whole race of the saints.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore also St. Paul saith, “Put on therefore, as the elect saints of God, bowels of mercy, kindness, humbleness of mind.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see the strict propriety of the word, and how he would have us continually merciful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he does not say, “show mercy” only, but put it on, that like as our garment is always with us, so may mercy be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not say merely mercy, but “bowels of mercy,” that we may imitate the natural affection of relations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But we do just the contrary, and if any one comes to ask a single penny of us, we insult them, abuse them, call them impostors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Dost thou not shudder, man, and blush to call him an impostor for bread?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why even supposing such an one is practising imposture, he deserves to be pitied for it, because he is so pressed with famine as to put on such a character.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then is a reproach to our cruelty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since we had not the heart to bestow with readiness, they are compelled to practise a great many arts, so as to put a cheat off upon our inhumanity, and to soften down our harshness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now if it was gold and silver that he asked of thee, then there would be some reason in thy suspicions.
